lastCompletedBlock.summary: Executed the approved broader Unity bootstrap tranche on-host: restored minimum repo-side Unity shell (`Packages/manifest.json`, `ProjectSettings/ProjectVersion.txt`), selected Unity 2022.3 LTS / 2022.3.70f1 bootstrap pin as the conservative compatible editor baseline, logged the first bootstrap-validation state, confirmed this Ubuntu host still lacks Unity/editor tooling, identified `libfuse2t64` as the minimum known sudo-needed package for the common Unity Hub AppImage path here, initialized Git with repo-local identity plus approved GitHub remote, committed the repo, and pushed `main` to origin.

lastScheduledBlock.jobName: Await approval/install of libfuse2t64, then continue Unity 2022.3 LTS import/EditMode validation on this host

readiness.blockingReason: The repo now has a minimum Unity project shell (`Packages/manifest.json`, `ProjectSettings/ProjectVersion.txt`) and a chosen 2022.3 LTS editor baseline, but first real compile/test validation on this Ubuntu host is still blocked because no Unity Editor/Hub is installed locally yet; the minimum known system dependency gap for the common AppImage path here is `libfuse2t64`, after which Unity 2022.3 LTS import and the first EditMode validation pass should be attempted before any wider work.

notes: Use STATUS.md for human-readable summary.
Use BACKLOG.md for priorities.
Use RUN_STATE.json for machine-readable orchestration context.

2026-03-13T21:24:00-07:00 human override approved broadening into the Unity bootstrap/tooling tranche on this host: restore/create minimum project bootstrap, choose conservative LTS-compatible editor, install minimum host tooling needed for first validation, and prefer execution over further doc-only planning.

2026-03-13T21:35:00-07:00 human override added Git/GitHub policy: initialize git if needed during the broader bootstrap tranche, use repo-local identity Sawyer Matzke <sawyermatzke@github.com>, and push coherent progress to git@github.com:sawyermatzke/flick_soccer.git.
2026-03-13T20:01:00-07:00 overnight safety-net de-duplicated overlapping review jobs and kept the earlier 20:05 review block to avoid duplicate work.

2026-03-13T20:23:00-07:00 autonomous cron delivery default changed to none to avoid misrouted announce delivery.
2026-03-13T20:26:00-07:00 pre-geometry architecture planning established semantic-authority multiplayer boundaries and Unity module seams without inventing coordinates.

2026-03-13T20:31:00-07:00 multiplayer stack evaluation favored server-authoritative semantic room state, with Nakama as the leading stack candidate and Photon Fusion retained as the main Unity-native fallback.

2026-03-13T20:33:00-07:00 room lifecycle analysis defined a server-owned private-room state machine, snapshot-based resume policy, and timeout/abandon/rematch rules; this reinforced the Nakama-first direction over host-dependent alternatives.

2026-03-13T20:36:00-07:00 sync-contract planning defined the room snapshot payload, reconnect acknowledgment and watermark flow, timeout/grace timer contract, and minimum resume/abandon/rematch command-event additions without crossing into geometry-dependent implementation.

2026-03-13T20:39:00-07:00 DTO boundary planning separated room-scoped vs match-scoped contracts/events, defined minimum snapshot/event/timer/watermark persistence shapes, and set rematch to create fresh match-scoped history while preserving room continuity.

2026-03-13T20:43:00-07:00 scaffolding planning defined the first safe Assets/Binho/Core folder map, DTO/type inventory, abstraction-first Core.Sync seams, and serialization/unit-test checklist for pre-geometry package creation.

2026-03-13T20:45:00-07:00 tranche planning narrowed the scaffold into a four-assembly first implementation slice with explicit file order, DTO priorities, in-memory adapter limits, and a focused test matrix for serialization, watermarks, rematch boundaries, and room-vs-match separation.

2026-03-13T20:47:00-07:00 checklist/fixture planning produced a dedicated coding handoff doc with phase-ordered file creation, namespace/asmdef guidance, concrete JSON fixtures, and an exact validation inventory for the first Core.Contracts/Core.Sync coding block.

2026-03-13T20:55:00-07:00 DTO-shell planning pinned exact day-one versus deferred fields, added minimal validator/helper pseudocode, and translated the first tranche into concrete test file/method naming and execution order.

2026-03-13T20:57:00-07:00 final ambiguity sweep removed the RoomLifecycleState-vs-RoomPhase naming drift, classified acknowledgeSnapshot/startMatch as room-lifecycle commands, moved TimerRecordScope into shared metadata, and assigned scope validators to a dedicated Core.Sync.Validation seam before implementation starts.

2026-03-13T21:09:00-07:00 first safe Core.Contracts/Core.Sync scaffold tranche implemented under Assets/Binho/Core with asmdefs, DTO shells, scope validators, watermark/resume helpers, in-memory adapters, and planned tests; automated compile/test execution remains pending because the current host lacks dotnet/C# and Unity runner tooling.

2026-03-13T21:12:00-07:00 review pass confirmed the scaffold still fits the clarified pre-geometry tranche, fixed a missing SnapshotAckHandler using/import for IClock resolution, and flagged production-assembly placement of TestClock/TestIdGenerator as the next repo-integration sanity check.

2026-03-13T21:15:00-07:00 repo-integration review moved TestClock/TestIdGenerator into the sync test assembly subtree, confirmed the asmdef graph still stays within the tranche boundary, and identified the next blocker as missing local Unity/C# tooling plus likely missing wider Unity project bootstrap context in this checkout.
